creator: linimon@lonesome.com
text:
----- Forwarded message from cbc <cbc@labmaster.com> -----

I have two identical machines: shuttle ST20G5 (uses the M5287 SATA controller)
Both machines can see the sata drives with FreeBsd 6.1, but cannot see 
it with FreeBSD 6.2
One machine is in use as a web server (FreeBSD 6.1)
I am using the other one to try and upgrade to FreeBSD 6.2, however I 
cannot load FreeBSD 6.2 on it as it fails to see the sata disk during 
installation. I have installed FreeBSD6.2 on a pata drive and was going 
to rebuild the kernel to see if updated code would help.
Now I think I should load 6.1 and try cvs upgrade to 6.2 and try it that 
way.
What do you think?


NS1 - boots FreeBSD 6.1, /var/log/messages: sata section
Jun 27 18:45:01 ns1 kernel: atapci0: <AcerLabs M5229 UDMA133 controller> 
port 0x1f0-0x1f7,0x3f6,0x170-0x177,0x376,0xfe00-0xfe0f at device 31.0
on pci0
Jun 27 18:45:01 ns1 kernel: ata0: <ATA channel 0> on atapci0
Jun 27 18:45:01 ns1 kernel: ata1: <ATA channel 1> on atapci0
Jun 27 18:45:01 ns1 kernel: atapci1: <AcerLabs M5287 SATA150 controller> 
port 0xfd00-0xfd0f,0xfc00-0xfc07,0xfb00-0xfb0f,0xfa00-0xfa07,0xf900-0x
f91f mem 0xdfffb000-0xdfffb3ff irq 19 at device 31.1 on pci0

Jun 27 18:45:01 ns1 kernel: ata3: <ATA channel 1> on atapci1
Jun 27 18:45:01 ns1 kernel: ata4: <ATA channel 2> on atapci1
